newsletters.title: How GPT-5.6 Changes Knowledge Work
newsletters.content:
DonвҖҷt do your work. Tend your loop. В вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢВ вҖҢ
https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEdQVC01LjYgQ2hhbmdlcyBLbm93bGVkZ2UgV29yayIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvLyIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjowfQ==

Chain of Thought

https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEdQVC01LjYgQ2hhbmdlcyBLbm93bGVkZ2UgV29yayIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL2NoYWluLW9mLXRob3VnaHQiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MX0=


HOW GPT-5.6 CHANGES KNOWLEDGE WORK https://every.


DONвҖҷT DO YOUR WORK. TEND YOUR LOOP. https://every.

by Dan Shipper https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEdQVC01LjYgQ2hhbmdlcyBLbm93bGVkZ2UgV29yayIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL0BkYW5zaGlwcGVyIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjR9

Midjourney/Every illustration.

TL;DR: GPT-5.6 is the first model IвҖҷve used that can reliably run whole loops of knowledge work, not just help with individual tasks. Your job turns from doing the work to tending the system that does it. If you want to try this for yourself, weвҖҷre open-sourcing a prompt and repository called Tend https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEdQVC01LjYgQ2hhbmdlcyBLbm93bGVkZ2UgV29yayIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL3RlbmQiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6NX0= so you can try working this way in ChatGPT Work.

Experiment with Tend https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEdQVC01LjYgQ2hhbmdlcyBLbm93bGVkZ2UgV29yayIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL3RlbmQ_c291cmNlPXBvc3RfYnV0dG9uIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjZ9



----------------------------------------



GPT-5.6 heralds a new way of doing knowledge work.

Instead of using AI to complete one task at a time, you build a system that scans the available information, turns it into proposed decisions, and carries out the ones you approve. Over time, the system compounds your feedback to do more and more on its own.

This changes your job. It requires you to see your work asвҖ”dare I say itвҖ”a loop https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEdQVC01LjYgQ2hhbmdlcyBLbm93bGVkZ2UgV29yayIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL2NvbnRleHQtd2luZG93L2xvb3BzLWZvci1ub24tY29kZXJzIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjd9. You go from doing all of the work yourself to tending the system that does it for you:

https://every. knowledge work loop, with human judgment at the center. (Image courtesy of Dan Shipper/Claude/GPT Image.)




Take email. I used to go through my inbox like this: Email comes in, I read it, I reply, I archive. (Or, email comes in, I open it, I close it, I wait several weeks, I open it again, I archive it.)

With GPT-5.6 Sol https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEdQVC01LjYgQ2hhbmdlcyBLbm93bGVkZ2UgV29yayIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL3ZpYmUtY2hlY2svZ3B0LTUtNiIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo5fQ== in the new ChatGPT Work app, formerly known as Codex, the process looks very different. GPT-5.6 Sol watches my inbox, decides what deserves my attention, does any necessary research, and presents each email with a concise summary and proposed reply. I either approve the draft or use Monologue https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEdQVC01LjYgQ2hhbmdlcyBLbm93bGVkZ2UgV29yayIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL3d3dy5tb25vbG9ndWUudG8vIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjEwfQ== to dictate what I want changed. Then I move to the next email.

At the end of each sweep through my inbox, the agent derives my preferences from my revisions and decisions and remembers them for next time.

ItвҖҷs no coincidence this sounds like compound engineering https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEdQVC01LjYgQ2hhbmdlcyBLbm93bGVkZ2UgV29yayIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL2d1aWRlcy9jb21wb3VuZC1lbmdpbmVlcmluZyIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxMX0=; itвҖҷs the same philosophyвҖ”just applied to knowledge work. IвҖҷve been writing about this shift for a few years: In early 2024 I argued that much knowledge work would become managing agents https://every., and later that it would look like tending to a garden https://every.вҖ”creating the conditions for work to happen, rather than doing everything yourself directly. This isnвҖҷt a new way of working: Managers and entrepreneurs have done it for decades, and as models improved over the past year, programmers adopted it too. Now itвҖҷs knowledge workвҖҷs turn.

This approach wonвҖҷt work for every kind of knowledge work, and itвҖҷs still early. But where it works, it creates a remarkable kind of leverage.


WHAT MAKES GPT-5.6 SOL AND CHATGPT WORK DIFFERENT

GPT-5.6 Sol crosses the threshold that makes a continuous knowledge-work loop practical. It can scan your sources, identify whatвҖҷs relevant, carry out approved work, and build custom tools for itself as needed. It can do all of this reliably even if you canвҖҷt codeвҖ”and it can explain all of this to you in a way thatвҖҷs understandable.

ItвҖҷs also fast and cheap enough that you can iterate rapidlyвҖ”a crucial requirement for non-technical users who are going to make mistakes and need to see the results of a run before knowing i

Together with

Caption: 



----------
howdy, itвҖҷs Barsee again.

happy friday, AI family, and welcome back to AI Valley.

**here are the biggest things worth knowing today:**

* OpenAIвҖҷs new вҖңSuper AppвҖқ wants to be your co-worker

* OpenAI just made ChatGPT conversations feel more human

* 1X unveiled one of the most advanced humanoid robot hands

* Muse Spark 1.1 debuts as MetaвҖҷs advanced coding model

* Plus trending AI tools, posts, and resources

LetвҖҷs _dive_ into the Valley of AIвҖҰ


----------

----------
#### **VOICES**

## [Stop wasting time sourcing voice training data. It already exists](https://www.voices.com/landing/off-the-shelf-catalogue?utm_source=ai_valley&utm_medium=newsletter&utm_campaign=client_ots-bulk-sales-play_2026)

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/3fdb9dec-7aa8-4f49-a439-0ef612f07879/AI-valley-v1__1_.png?t=1783694210)
Follow image link: (https://www.voices.com/landing/off-the-shelf-catalogue?utm_source=ai_valley&utm_medium=newsletter&utm_campaign=client_ots-bulk-sales-play_2026)
Caption: Courtesy: Voices

Building production-grade AI voice starts long before model trainingвҖ”it starts with sourcing, licensing, and structuring your data. That part shouldn't take months.

The Voices Dataset Catalogue gives AI and ML teams immediate access to professionally directed Voice Data: character performances spanning 450+ unique types, expressive speech across 43 emotional states, and multilingual recordings across 8 languages (part of 100,000+ hours of premium Voice Data available from Voices).

Every dataset is fully consented, production-ready, and structured for immediate pipeline integration.

Three specialized datasets. Real range. Zero sourcing headaches.

[Request a Sample вҶ’](https://www.voices.com/landing/off-the-shelf-catalogue?utm_source=ai_valley&utm_medium=newsletter&utm_campaign=client_ots-bulk-sales-play_2026)

~_*This is sponsored_~


----------

----------
#### **THROUGH THE VALLEY**

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/819080f2-3abe-4b50-9e42-55b62ec48eb0/image_-_2026-07-10T200328.714__1_.png?t=1783694422)
Caption: Courtesy: Anthropic

**1/ OpenAIвҖҷs new вҖңSuper AppвҖқ wants to be your co-worker: **The company launched [ChatGPT Work](https://openai.com/index/chatgpt-for-your-most-ambitious-work/), a GPT-5.6-powered workspace that combines chat, coding, automation, and workplace integrations to complete complex tasks with minimal supervision. Connected to tools like Slack, Microsoft Teams, Google Drive, SharePoint, and Salesforce, it can work for hours on multi-step projects, bringing OpenAI into direct competition with Anthropic's Claude Cowork and Microsoft Copilot. ([watch the announcement live stream here](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Wq45rvPGNHs)_)_.

**2/ OpenAI just made ChatGPT conversations feel more human:** The company launched [GPT-Live](https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-live), a new full-duplex voice model that allows ChatGPT to listen and speak simultaneously, enabling natural interruptions, faster responses, and more fluid back-and-forth conversations. The new experience also supports real-time interruptions, memory, search, and GPT-5.5-powered responses, bringing voice interactions closer to speaking with another person. ([watch the full livestream here](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9f-Ew_lDtxc))

**3/ GPT-5.6 rolls out broadly as OpenAIвҖҷs new top AI model:** The company [launched](https://openai.com/index/gpt-5-6) GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna, a new family of models built for coding, reasoning, cybersecurity, and enterprise workloads. OpenAI says its flagship Sol outperforms Anthropic's Fable 5 while running faster, using 54% fewer tokens on agentic coding tasks, and costing roughly one-third less. GPT-5.6 Sol has been [ranked 2nd](https://x.com/ArtificialAnlys/status/2075268970492657905) to AnthropicвҖҷs Claude Fable 5 on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index. ([watch the launch here](https://x.com/OpenAI/status/2075271421149020426?s=20))

**4/ Anthropic is turning AI usage into something you can measure: **The company launched a new [reflection dashboard](https://x.com/claudeai/status/2075225957711929667?s=20) for Claude that analyzes conversations across recurring topics, task types, and usage patterns over the past year. Alongside privacy-focused insights, the feature adds quiet hours, break reminders, and self-reflection prompts to help users build healthier AI habits.

**5/ 1X just unveiled one of the most advanced humanoid robot hands yet: **The company's new [NEO hand](https://x.com/1x_tech/status/2075252899442204952?s=20) features 25 degrees of freedom, force sensing in every joint, tactile skin, and a lightweight tendon-driven design that closely mimics human dexterity. Built to be waterproof, durable, and mass-manufacturable, 1X says it already has the capacity to produce up to 10,000 hands this year.

**6/ SpaceXAI and Cursor just launched Grok's biggest upgrade yet:** The companies unveiled [Grok 4.5](https://x.ai/news/grok-4-5), a new flagship model that delivers Opus-class performance across coding, agentic, and knowledge tasks while running significantly faster and at a fraction of the cost. With benchmark results approaching Claude Opus 4.8 and GPT-5.5, Grok 4.5 positions itself as one of the strongest value-focused frontier AI models available.

**7/ Muse Spark 1.1 debuts as MetaвҖҷs advanced coding model:** The company launched [Muse Spark 1.1](https://x.com/alexandr_wang/status/2075218936266998230?s=20) alongside the public preview of the Meta Model API, bringing a multimodal reasoning model with a 1 million-token context window, advanced agent workflows, and stronger coding capabilities. Meta says the model delivers major improvements over its predecessor while enabling developers to build AI agents that can use tools, browse apps, and complete complex tasks autonomously.


----------

----------
# TRENDING TOOLS


--------------------
* [Reve 2.1](https://x.com/reve/status/2075248950756716747?s=20) > The world's leading 4K image generation model just got even better

* [GPT-Live](https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-live) > OpenAI's new full duplex voice experience for ChatGPT enables natural, real time conversations with minimal latency

* [Notion Ship OS](https://x.com/NotionHQ/status/2075249002208227743) > Notion introduced Ship OS, an agent native workspace designed to help teams plan, build, and ship software faster

* [Ora Directory](https://directory.ora.ai/) > A searchable directory of tools, APIs, and resources that AI agents can discover and use

* [ChatGPT Work](https://openai.com/chatgpt-work) > A GPT-5.6 powered work agent that can plan tasks, browse the web, use connectors, generate deliverables, schedule work, and seamlessly continue across desktop and mobile

* [Muse Spark 1](https://ai.meta.com/blog/introducing-muse-spark-meta-model-api) > Meta unveiled Spark 1, its latest multimodal reasoning model built for advanced AI understanding and problem solving

* [Claude Cowork](https://x.com/claudeai/status/20745

Vibe Check

https://every.


VIBE CHECK: GPT-5.6 SOL IS OUR FAVORITE MODEL TO COLLABORATE WITH https://every.


SOL IS FAST, RESOURCEFUL, AND UNUSUALLY EASY TO STEERвҖ”BUT FABLE STILL GETS THE ASSIGNMENTS WE WANT TO HAND OFF COMPLETELY https://every.

by Katie Parrott https://every.

Midjourney/Every illustration.

This is a free preview of a subscribers-only post.

Was this newsletter forwarded to you? Sign up to get it in your inbox.



----------------------------------------



At the end of June, we lost access to GPT-5.6 Sol while it went through government review. Dan Shipper said returning to older models felt like going back to the Stone Age. Austin Tedesco compared GPT-5.5 to shooting a basketball twice as heavy.

Now Sol is back. ItвҖҷs become our default for work we want to shape as we go: writing, coding, research, and analysis inside OpenAIвҖҷs new unified ChatGPT and Codex desktop app. Sol is fast, unusually good at finding and using context, and easy to redirect. AnthropicвҖҷs Fable still leads on broad assignments that require the model to decide what should be built in the first place.

We get into all of it in our Vibe Check, including:

 * Why Sol scored 56/100 against FableвҖҷs 90/100 on our Senior Engineer benchmarkвҖ”and how 12,900 lines of unnecessary code explain most of the gap
 * How Kieran Klaassen rebuilt Proof with Sol in about one-third the time Fable needed, only to prefer FableвҖҷs design
 * Why Sol finished last in our six-model writing benchmark while I (Katie Parrott) still used it to move through 24 drafts of this article in six to eight hours
 * How Sol found Arielle ShipperвҖҷs email, inspected 46 CSV files, and returned with seven useful questionsвҖ”then made a calculation error serious enough to shake her trust


THIS POST IS FOR
PAYING SUBSCRIBERS.

newsletters.title: Want to join us in Brooklyn?
newsletters.content:
вҖӢ

( https://0ab9ee3d.click.kit-mail3.com/xmu6zrxe6wf6hp520zxt5h2eo56r3unh8lr4l/kkhmh6hn0vlwwvclh7/aHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50bw== )

Our July IRL meetup is for paid Every subscribers.
Hi there,

We're bringing the Every community together outside for another summer evening in the backyard of our Brooklyn brownstone.

It'll be a casual hang with readers, writers, founders, operators, and creative people thinking seriously about AI, work, ideas, and what they're building next.

Every IRLвҖ”July 2026
Wednesday, July 15
6вҖ“8 p.m. ET
Brooklyn, NY

This event is for paid subscribers. Upgrade to request a spot.

Subscribe and RSVP ( https://0ab9ee3d.click.kit-mail3.com/xmu6zrxe6wf6hp520zxt5h2eo56r3unh8lr4l/58hvh7hgd2mxx8u6h4/aHR0cHM6Ly9sdW1hLmNvbS9oc2t6ejJiMQ== )

Hope to see you outside,
The Every team

newsletters.title: Welcome to Efficiencymaxxing

by Laura Entis https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiV2VsY29tZSB0byBFZmZpY2llbmN5bWF4eGluZyIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzEsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL0BsYXVyYV8yN2JiYWZfMSIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo0fQ==

https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiV2VsY29tZSB0byBFZmZpY2llbmN5bWF4eGluZyIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzEsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL3d3dy55b3V0dWJlLmNvbS93YXRjaD92PTdORDBsUW1MSmxBIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjV9 Midjourney/Every illustration.

For a brief period, AI was an affordable novelty. Every use case felt like magic, and even the silliest task was worth a try when frontier labs were subsidizing compute costs to get consumers hooked. Power users proved their status by maxing out their token consumption https://every..

Now, AI is ubiquitousвҖ”even required in many workplacesвҖ”and easy to use for anything from code to text to visuals. But this flood of production has a price: in cash, as powerful new models https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiV2VsY29tZSB0byBFZmZpY2llbmN5bWF4eGluZyIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzEsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL2NoYWluLW9mLXRob3VnaHQvdGhlLW1vcmFsLW9mLWZhYmxlIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjd9 grow more token-hungry https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiV2VsY29tZSB0byBFZmZpY2llbmN5bWF4eGluZyIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzEsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL2NvbnRleHQtd2luZG93L3Rva2VuLXRpZ2h0ZW5pbmciLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6OH0= and the labs roll back those generous subsidies, but also in the time and effort required to make sense of the results. (If youвҖҷve ever tried to debug an AI-generated codebase https://every., edit AI-generated text https://every., or decipher the meaning behind an AI-generated email https://every., you know how labor-intensive it can be to wade through poor-quality LLM outputs.)

Focus has evolved accordingly from how much youвҖҷre using AI to how youвҖҷre using itвҖ”and what you can show for it. Does the benefit justify the significant cost?

TodayвҖҷs Context Window explores various answers and solutions to that question. First up, author and technologist Craig Mod https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiV2VsY29tZSB0byBFZmZpY2llbmN5bWF4eGluZyIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzEsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2NyYWlnbW9kLmNvbS8iLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MTJ9 explains why cheap software creation has made him more protective of his writing time; Monologue https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiV2VsY29tZSB0byBFZmZpY2llbmN5bWF4eGluZyIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzEsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL3d3dy5tb25vbG9ndWUudG8vP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZXZlcnl3ZWJzaXRlIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjEzfQ== general manager Naveen Naidu https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiV2VsY29tZSB0byBFZmZpY2llbmN5bWF4eGluZyIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzEsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL0BuYXZlZW5fNjgwNCIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxNH0= shares a new efficiency metric he heard making the rounds in San Francisco; senior applied AI engineer Nityesh Agarwal https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiV2VsY29tZSB0byBFZmZpY2llbmN5bWF4eGluZyIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzEsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL0BuaXR5ZXNoIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjE1fQ== shows us how he audits agents for wasted tokens; and Spiral https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiV2VsY29tZSB0byBFZmZpY2llbmN5bWF4eGluZyIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzEsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL3dyaXRld2l0aHNwaXJhbC5jb20vP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZXZlcnl3ZWJzaXRlIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjE2fQ== general manager Marcus Moretti https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiV2VsY29tZSB0byBFZmZpY2llbmN5bWF4eGluZyIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzEsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL0BtYXJjdXNfZmQ4MzAyXzEiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MTd9 explains how OpenRouter helps him manage a 12-plus-model stack.
